componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring properties of an Alert component.
componentInterfaces.name: IAlertProps

members.description: An optional boolean flag indicating whether the alert should be filled. When set to true, the component is filled with the specified status tone. Default value is false.
members.name: isFilled

members.description: An optional boolean determining whether the alert component has sharp edges. Default value is false.
members.name: isSharp

members.description: An optional color variant of the alert, typically used to classify the information based on its level (e.g., success, warning, error, etc.). Default value is info.
members.name: status

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an Avatar component.
componentInterfaces.name: IAvatarProps

members.description: An optional property that allows specifying the border color for the avatar.
members.name: accent

members.description: An optional property that determines the size (girth) of the avatar.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: An optional property that provides a custom JSX icon element to be rendered within the avatar if no image source (src) is provided.
members.name: icon

members.description: An optional attribute employed for the generation of initials, which holds precedence over the icon property.
members.name: name

members.description: An optional attribute that precisely designates the image source for the avatar, and it takes precedence over both the name and icon properties.
members.name: src

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an AvatarBadge component.
componentInterfaces.name: IAvatarBadgeProps

members.description: An optional attribute that enables the specification of the border color for the avatar badge.
members.name: accent

members.description: An optional attribute that decisively defines the tone of the avatar badge.
members.name: tone

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an AvatarGroup component.
componentInterfaces.name: IAvatarGroupProps

members.description: An optional property that determines the size (girth) of the avatar group.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: An optional property that specifies the maximum number of avatars allowed within the group.
members.name: max

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a Badge component.
componentInterfaces.name: IBadgeProps

members.description: An optional property dictating the content to be showcased within the badge.
members.name: content

members.description: An optional property determining whether the badge should exhibit blinking behavior, following a linear 1-second interval.
members.name: isBlink

members.description: An optional property specifying whether the badge should manifest as a dot without any associated content.
members.name: isDotted

members.description: An optional property indicating whether the badge should appear as an outline rather than being filled.
members.name: isOutlined

members.description: An optional property compelling the badge to display the numeral '0', even when the data value is precisely 0. Applicable solely to numeric content.
members.name: isShow0

members.description: An optional property establishing the maximum allowable integer value within the badge. If the data value exceeds this cap, a + suffix is appended. Relevant exclusively to numeric content.
members.name: max

members.description: An optional property defining the visual tone of the badge component, influencing its aesthetic representation.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a Border component.
componentInterfaces.name: IBorderProps

members.description: An optional property used to adjust the content placement within the border, supporting both positive and negative values.
members.name: align

members.description: An optional JSX element that resides within the border.
members.name: content

members.description: An optional property specifying the border width.
members.name: density

members.description: An optional property that determines whether sharp borders should be created.
members.name: isSharp

members.description: An optional property specifying the positioning of the content within the border.
members.name: positioning
members.types: top-left | top-center | top-right | right-top | right-center | right-bottom | bottom-right | bottom-center | bottom-left | left-bottom | left-center | left-top

members.description: An optional property defining the shade of the border.
members.name: shade

members.description: An optional property specifying the color tone of the border.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: An optional property defining the border style.
members.name: variant
members.types: dashed | dotted | double | groove | hidden | ridge | solid

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a Breadcrumb component.
componentInterfaces.name: IBreadcrumbProps

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the last item in the breadcrumb is set as inactive.
members.name: isLastItemNotActive

members.description: An optional property that limits the number of items displayed within the breadcrumb.
members.name: limit

members.description: An optional callback function triggered when the collapsed breadcrumb is expanded.
members.name: onExpand

members.description: An optional property defining the separator used between breadcrumb items. The default separator is '/'.
members.name: separator

members.description: An optional property that sets the visual tone for the appearance of the BreadcrumbItem.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an individual BreadcrumbItem component.
componentInterfaces.name: IBreadcrumbItemProps

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the current breadcrumb item is set as active.
members.name: isActive

members.description: An optional property that sets the visual tone for the appearance of the breadcrumb item. The default tone is neutral.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a Button component.
componentInterfaces.name: IButtonProps

members.description: An optional property that determines the size of the button.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: An optional property allowing embedding an icon within the button.
members.name: icon

members.description: An optional property specifying whether the button should have a gradient tone.
members.name: isGradient

members.description: An optional property determining whether the button is disabled, blocked for actions, and shows a loader.
members.name: isLoading

members.description: An optional property specifying whether the button stretches to full width.
members.name: isStretched

members.description: An optional property specifying whether the text content inside the button is displayed in uppercase.
members.name: isUppercase

members.description: An optional property specifying the color tone of the button.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description:
An optional property specifying the variant style of the button.

primary -> For most significant actions in a page. If at all, only one recommended in a page
outlined -> Second most significant actions, can be multiple
hero -> Like a banner on a page, use this if you want to standout the action you want the user to take
basic -> All other actions
simple -> For ghost actions, use this if you don't want to let user notice that it exists
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an Anchor component.
componentInterfaces.name: IAnchorProps

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the anchor should open the associated link in a new tab.
members.name: canLaunch

members.description: An optional property that determines the size of the anchor component.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: An optional property that specifies the color tone of the anchor component.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring an IconButton component.
componentInterfaces.name: IIconButtonProps

members.description: An optional property that determines the size of the icon button component.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: A required property representing the mandatory icon embedded inside the button.
members.name: icon

members.description: An optional property that specifies whether the button is circular and contains only an icon.
members.name: isIconOnly

members.description: An optional property that specifies the placement of the icon inside the button.
members.name: placement

members.description: An optional property that specifies the color tone of the icon button component.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: An optional property that specifies the variant style of the icon button component.
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a ButtonGroup component.
componentInterfaces.name: IButtonGroupProps

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the button group component stretches to full width, evenly distributing the width of the buttons.
members.name: isStretched

members.description: An optional property that specifies whether buttons inside the group are visually unified, presenting them as a single component. This can have a significant impact, especially in the case of an outlined button group.
members.name: isUnified

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the button group is vertically aligned.
members.name: isVertical

componentInterfaces.description: Interface defining properties for configuring a ButtonCluster component.
componentInterfaces.name: IButtonClusterProps

members.description: An optional property that determines whether the button cluster component stretches to full width, evenly distributing the width of the buttons.
members.name: isStretched

componentInterfaces.description: The ICardProps interface provides a set of properties that can be used to customize and enhance the appearance and behavior of a card component. Cards are versatile elements that can hold various types of content, such as text, images, and links, and are commonly used to present information in a visually appealing and organized manner.
componentInterfaces.name: ICardProps

members.description: Determines the accent color variant of the card, which is typically used to categorize or classify information based on its importance or level. This property allows you to add a touch of visual emphasis to the card's content.
members.name: accent
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: Specifies the position of the accent color within the card. This property lets you control the placement of the accent color, enhancing the card's visual hierarchy.
members.name: accentPosition

members.description: Enables the card to function as a hyperlink. By providing a URL string, you can turn the entire card into a clickable link, allowing users to navigate to a specified destination when the card is clicked.
members.name: href

members.description: Determines whether the card should have sharp edges. When set to true, the card's corners will be sharp, creating a distinct visual style.
members.name: isSharp

componentInterfaces.description: The ICardFooterProps interface offers a set of properties that allow you to enhance and customize the footer section of a card component. Card footers are valuable components used to provide additional information, actions, or context related to the content within the card. With these properties, you can easily tailor the appearance and behavior of the card footer to align with your application's design and user experience goals.
componentInterfaces.name: ICardFooterProps

members.description: This property controls whether the footer of the card is separated from the main body by a divider. When set to true, a subtle line is added to visually distinguish the footer from the rest of the card content. This option is particularly useful when you want to create a clear visual distinction between the footer and the main content of the card.
members.name: isDivided

componentInterfaces.description: The ICardHeaderProps interface provides a set of properties that allow you to enhance and customize the header section of a card component. Card headers are essential elements that can contain titles, captions, or other information to provide context and introduce the content within the card. With these properties, you can easily tailor the appearance and behavior of the card header to align with your application's design and user experience goals.
componentInterfaces.name: ICardHeaderProps

members.description: This property enables you to create a hyperlink within the card header. When a valid URL is provided, a launch icon is added to the top right corner of the header. Clicking on the launch icon opens the associated hyperlink in a new browser tab, providing users with a convenient way to access external content related to the card's context.
members.name: href

members.description: This property controls whether the header of the card is separated from the main body by a divider. When set to true, a subtle line is added to visually distinguish the header from the rest of the card content. This option can be useful when you want to create a clear visual separation between the header and the main content of the card.
members.name: isDivided

componentInterfaces.description: The ICardBodyProps interface provides a set of properties that allow you to customize and optimize the main content section of a card component. The card body is where you can present the core information, media, or textual content that users engage with. With these properties, you have the flexibility to tailor the appearance and behavior of the card body to match your application's design and user experience objectives.
componentInterfaces.name: ICardBodyProps

componentInterfaces.description: The ICheckboxProps interface represents the properties that can be passed to a checkbox component in your application.
componentInterfaces.name: ICheckboxProps

members.description: Specifies the size or thickness of the checkbox.
members.name: girth

members.description: An optional boolean flag indicating whether the checkbox should be filled. When set to true, the component is filled with the specified status tone.
members.name: isFilled

members.description: Indicates whether the checkbox is in an indeterminate state. An indeterminate checkbox is neither fully selected nor empty. If true, the checkbox is rendered as indeterminate; if false or not provided, it follows the normal selected or empty state.
members.name: isIndeterminate

members.description: An optional color variant of the checkbox, typically used to classify the information based on its level (e.g., success, warning, error, etc.). Default value is info.
members.name: status

componentInterfaces.description: The IChipProps interface provides a comprehensive set of properties that can be utilized to customize and control the behavior of the Chip component. The Chip is a versatile UI element designed to display compact pieces of information or actions, often used for tags, labels, or interactive items.
componentInterfaces.name: IChipProps

members.description: The girth property controls the size of the chip component. You can use predefined size values, such as 'sm', 'md', 'lg', etc., to adjust the visual dimensions of the chip.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: The icon property allows you to embed an icon element inside the chip. This icon can enhance the visual representation of the chip and provide additional context or visual cues.
members.name: icon

members.description: When set to true, the isClickable property enables click actions on the chip component. This means that users can interact with the chip by clicking on it, triggering any associated actions or behaviors.
members.name: isClickable

members.description: The isFilled property determines whether the chip should have a filled variant. When isFilled is set to true, the chip will have a visually distinct filled appearance, which can help emphasize its presence or state.
members.name: isFilled

members.description: The placement property controls the positioning of the icon element inside the chip. You can choose between 'left' and 'right' to specify whether the icon should be displayed to the left or right of the chip's content.
members.name: placement

members.description: The tone property defines the color tone of the chip component. You can use predefined tone values like 'major', 'minor', etc., to apply different color schemes to the chip. The default tone is 'neutral'.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: The ICodeProps interface is designed to provide you with fine-grained control over the appearance and behavior of a code snippet component.
componentInterfaces.name: ICodeProps

members.description: The canCopy property enables you to control the presence of a convenient copy-to-clipboard button within the code snippet component. When canCopy is set to true (the default), users can easily copy the code snippet to their clipboard with a single click. However, if you wish to restrict this functionality, you can set canCopy to false.
members.name: canCopy

componentInterfaces.description: The IContainerProps interface empowers you to customize the behavior and appearance of Containers within your user interface. These Containers serve as versatile wrappers for your content, helping you achieve consistent and visually pleasing layouts.
componentInterfaces.name: IContainerProps

members.description: The gutter property allows you to establish a comfortable breathing space around your container content. By defining a string value, you can determine the left and right padding, ensuring your content remains nicely spaced and easy on the eyes.
members.name: gutter

members.description: The isFixed property introduces responsiveness to your containers. When set to true, your container's maximum width will automatically adjust according to different device breakpoints. This ensures a seamless experience across various screen sizes.
members.name: isFixed

members.description: The maxWidth property enables you to define the maximum width of your container explicitly. By utilizing the TScreen type, you can ensure that your container maintains a consistent width while catering to your specific layout requirements.
members.name: maxWidth

componentInterfaces.description: The IDividerProps interface is your gateway to crafting elegant and customizable dividers that seamlessly integrate into your user interface. With a host of options to choose from, you can effortlessly create dividers that enhance both the visual appeal and user experience of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IDividerProps

members.description: The align property allows you to control the vertical alignment of your divider. Whether you want it centered, aligned at the start, or at the end, this property ensures that your divider blends seamlessly with the surrounding elements.
members.name: align

members.description: The density property lets you determine the thickness of your divider. Choose from predefined density values like 'thin', 'medium', or 'thick' to achieve the desired visual impact.
members.name: density

members.description: The isVertical property introduces vertical dividers to your arsenal. When set to true, this property allows you to create dividers that separate content vertically, adding depth and organization to your layout.
members.name: isVertical

members.description: The length property provides control over the height (for vertical dividers) or width (for horizontal dividers) of your divider. Use this property to fine-tune the dimensions of your divider to perfectly match your design.
members.name: length

members.description: With the shade property, you can adjust the color intensity of your divider. Choose from a range of shade values, from low to high, to seamlessly integrate the divider into your chosen color scheme.
members.name: shade

members.description: The tone property enables you to set the color tone of your divider. Whether you opt for a 'major', 'minor', or etc. tone, your divider will align harmoniously with your design language.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: The variant property lets you experiment with different divider styles. Choose from options like 'solid', 'dashed', 'dotted' etc. to achieve the desired visual effect.
members.name: variant
members.types: dashed | dotted | double | groove | hidden | ridge | solid

componentInterfaces.description: IEnhancedDividerProps interface – a treasure trove of properties that allow you to craft stunning dividers that enhance both the aesthetics and organization of your user interface. With an array of options at your fingertips, you have the power to create dividers that truly stand out.
componentInterfaces.name: IEnhancedDividerProps

members.description: The density property empowers you to control the thickness of your divider. Choose from preset density values such as 'thin', 'medium', or 'thick' to achieve the desired visual impact and separation.
members.name: density

members.description: The justify property gives you precise control over the horizontal alignment of your divider. Whether you want it centered, aligned at the start, or at the end, this property ensures that your divider seamlessly fits within its intended context.
members.name: justify

members.description: With the shade property, you have the ability to fine-tune the color intensity of your divider. Choose from a range of shade values, from subtle to bold, to perfectly match the tone of your application's color scheme.
members.name: shade

members.description: The tone property lets you set the color tone of your divider. Whether it's a 'major', 'minor' etc. tone, your divider will effortlessly blend into your design language while adding a touch of visual distinction.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: The variant property provides you with the ability to experiment with different divider styles. Choose from options like 'solid', 'dashed', 'dotted' etc. to achieve the perfect visual effect for your specific design.
members.name: variant
members.types: dashed | dotted | double | groove | hidden | ridge | solid

componentInterfaces.description: The IDrawerProps interface empowers you to design drawers that gracefully slide into view, offering convenient access to supplementary content or actions. Whether you're looking to enhance user experience or provide additional information, the IDrawerProps interface has you covered.
componentInterfaces.name: IDrawerProps

members.description: The isOpen property is your gatekeeper to the drawer's visibility. Set it to true, and behold as the drawer gracefully reveals its contents. A value of false ensures a clutter-free interface, neatly concealing the drawer.
members.name: isOpen

members.description: The onClose property lets you define a callback function that springs into action when users bid farewell to the drawer. This interactive feature enhances usability, making the drawer an intuitive and seamless part of your application.
members.name: onClose

componentInterfaces.description: The IFlexBoxProps interface opens the door to creating versatile and adaptable layouts using the power of flexbox. Whether you're aligning, justifying, or wrapping content, this interface puts the control in your hands.
componentInterfaces.name: IFlexBoxProps

members.description: Similar to CSS 'align-content', this property aligns flex lines within the flex container when there's extra space.
members.name: alc
members.types: normal | baseline | first baseline | last baseline | space-between | space-around | space-evenly | stretch | unsafe | safe | center | start | end | flex-start | flex-end

members.description: Similar to CSS align-items, this property aligns items along the cross axis within the flex container.
members.name: ali
members.types: normal | stretch | baseline | first baseline | last baseline | safe | unsafe | flex-start | flex-end | center | start | end | self-start | self-end

members.description: Equivalent to CSS column-gap, this property controls the gap between columns in a multi-column layout.
members.name: cgp

members.description: If set to true, centers the items both horizontally and vertically within the flex container. It also sets align-content and justify-content to center. If no height is provided, it stretches to full height.
members.name: ctr

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flex-direction, this property controls the direction in which flex items are placed within the flex container.
members.name: fld

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flow, this property controls the flow of content within a flex container.
members.name: flow

members.description: If set to true, sets the FlexBox to full height, expanding to the entire available vertical space.
members.name: fuh

members.description: Equivalent to CSS gap, this property controls the gap between flex items.
members.name: gap

members.description: Similar to CSS justify-content, this property aligns items along the main axis within the flex container.
members.name: jsc
members.types: center | start | end | flex-start | flex-end | left | right | normal | space-between | space-around | space-evenly | stretch | safe | unsafe

members.description: If set to true, prevents flex items from wrapping to the next line, forcing them to stay in a single line.
members.name: nowrap

members.description: Equivalent to CSS row-gap, this property controls the gap between rows in a multi-row layout.
members.name: rgp

componentInterfaces.description: The IFlexItemProps interface opens the door to creating flexible and adaptive content within your layouts. Whether you're fine-tuning alignment, controlling growth, or setting the order of flex items, this interface puts the control at your fingertips.
componentInterfaces.name: IFlexItemProps

members.description: Equivalent to CSS align-self, this property allows you to customize the alignment of a specific flex item within its container.
members.name: als
members.types: auto | normal | stretch | baseline | first baseline | last baseline | unsafe | safe | center | start | end | self-start | self-end | flex-start | flex-end

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flex-basis, this property sets the initial main size of a flex item before additional space is distributed.
members.name: fbs

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flex-grow, this property controls how much a flex item grows in relation to other flex items within the same container.
members.name: fgo

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flex, this property combines the flex-grow, flex-shrink, and flex-basis properties into a single value.
members.name: flx

members.description: Equivalent to CSS flex-shrink, this property controls how much a flex item shrinks in relation to other flex items within the same container.
members.name: fsk

members.description: If set to true, the FlexItem expands to full width within its container, utilizing the entire available horizontal space.
members.name: fuw

members.description: Equivalent to CSS order, this property allows you to control the order in which flex items appear within the container.
members.name: ord

members.description: Controls the width of the flex item within a FlexBox, ranging from 1/24th to 100% width. Values ranging from 1 to 24
members.name: span

componentInterfaces.description: The IFrameProps interface offers a versatile framework for creating visually appealing frames that encapsulate your content. Whether you're aiming for sharp edges, circular elegance, or streamlined wrapping, this interface provides the tools you need to achieve your design objectives.
componentInterfaces.name: IFrameProps

members.description: Indicate that the content is loaded and ready to be rendered, allowing the frame to gracefully transition or disappear.
members.name: isLoaded

members.description: Craft frames with sharp edges, adding a touch of modern aesthetics to your design.
members.name: isSharp

members.description: Opt to wrap child items neatly within the dimensions of the frame, ensuring a visually polished presentation.
members.name: isWrapped

members.description: Define the size (radius) of circular variants, enabling you to tailor the frame's appearance to your desired specifications.
members.name: size

members.description: Select the frame variant that suits your design vision. Choose between a circular variant ('circle') or a sleek line variant ('line'). If not supplied anything, it creates a rectangle box frame with given dimensions.
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: The IGridProps interface provides you with powerful tools to create dynamic and visually appealing grids in your projects. Whether you're designing complex layouts or simple grids, the properties offered by IGridProps will help you achieve your desired look and functionality.
componentInterfaces.name: IGridProps

members.description: This property adds half the specified value as negative left and right margins to the grid's rows. Additionally, it adds half the value as padding to each item within the grid.
members.name: gap

members.description: Customize the left and right padding of the entire grid.
members.name: gutter

members.description: Set this property to 'true' if you want to create a responsive grid that adjusts its width based on device breakpoints.
members.name: isFixed

componentInterfaces.description: The IItemProps interface is offering you a wide range of tools to craft engaging and flexible grid items. Whether you're designing for large desktop screens or tiny mobile devices, the properties provided by IItemProps will help you seamlessly adjust the spanning of your grid items.
componentInterfaces.name: IItemProps

members.description: Set the spanning of the grid item for desktop screens.
members.name: lg

members.description: Adjust the spanning of the grid item for laptops.
members.name: md

members.description: Define the spanning of the grid item for tablets.
members.name: sm

members.description: Specify the spanning of the grid item for TVs.
members.name: xl

members.description: Set the spanning of the grid item for mobile devices.
members.name: xs

componentInterfaces.description: The ITextInputProps interface is providing you with a wide array of features to create interactive and visually appealing text input components. Whether you're designing simple form fields or complex data entry points, the properties offered by ITextInputProps will help you craft user-friendly and functional inputs.
componentInterfaces.name: ITextInputProps

members.description: Add a descriptive caption below the text input component to provide additional context or guidance to users.
members.name: caption

members.description: Determine the girth (thickness) of the text input component, influencing its visual appearance and prominence.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: If set to true, the text input component will stretch to occupy the full width of its container.
members.name: isStretched

members.description: Assign a color variant to the text input component, typically used to convey different levels of information or status.
members.name: status

members.description: Specify the visual variant of the text input component, influencing its appearance, such as whether it's outlined or filled etc.
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: The IInputGroupProps interface is offering you a variety of properties to create well-structured and cohesive input group components. Whether you're designing a complex form or a simple input arrangement, the properties provided by IInputGroupProps will help you achieve a seamless and organized user experience.
componentInterfaces.name: IInputGroupProps

members.description: Determine the girth (thickness) of the input group component, influencing its visual appearance and prominence.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: Set this property to true if you want the elements within the input group to blend harmoniously, creating a cohesive and integrated look.
members.name: isBlend

componentInterfaces.description: The IInputElementProps interface equips you with a range of properties to craft interactive and visually appealing input elements. Whether you're creating buttons, icons, or other interactive components, the properties offered by IInputElementProps will empower you to seamlessly integrate dynamic elements into your projects.
componentInterfaces.name: IInputElementProps

members.description: Define the girth (thickness) of the input element, influencing its visual appearance and prominence.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: When set to true, elements within the input component blend harmoniously, creating a cohesive and integrated look.
members.name: isBlend

members.description: Set this property to true to make the element clickable, allowing user interaction.
members.name: isClickable

componentInterfaces.description: The ILoaderProps interface is providing you with a powerful toolkit to design versatile and dynamic loader components. Whether you're indicating progress, waiting for data, or simply adding a touch of flair, the properties offered by ILoaderProps will help you craft loader experiences that captivate your users.
componentInterfaces.name: ILoaderProps

members.description: Control the size of the loader, influencing its visual appearance and prominence.
members.name: girth

members.description: Choose from different variants to determine the loader's style. Options include a circular loader, ellipses animation, or a pulsating effect.
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: The IModalProps interface equipping you with a robust set of properties to create dynamic and user-friendly modal components. Whether you're displaying additional information, capturing user input, or providing context, the properties offered by IModalProps will help you design modals that enhance user experience.
componentInterfaces.name: IModalProps

members.description: Define a callback function that triggers when the backdrop of the modal is clicked. This allows you to implement custom behavior when the user interacts with the backdrop area.
members.name: onBackdropClick

componentInterfaces.description: The INoteProps interface is providing you with a versatile toolkit to create engaging and visually appealing note components. Whether you're highlighting key information, adding a touch of realism, or simply conveying a message, the properties offered by INoteProps will help you craft note components that stand out.
componentInterfaces.name: INoteProps

members.description: Enhance the note's visual impact by applying reality effects. When set to true, the note acquires a realistic appearance that engages users
members.name: isReal

componentInterfaces.description: The INotificationProps interface provides you with a robust set of properties to design dynamic and user-centric notification components. Whether you're delivering alerts, updates, or important messages, the properties offered by INotificationProps will help you design notifications that effectively engage your users.
componentInterfaces.name: INotificationProps

members.description: Control whether a label is added to the notification widget. When set to false, the label is omitted.
members.name: hasLabel

members.description: Create a filled notification widget by setting this property to true, altering its visual appearance.
members.name: isFilled

members.description: Achieve sharp corners for the notification widget by setting this property to true, offering a distinct visual style.
members.name: isSharp

members.description: Assign a color variant to the notification, typically indicating the information level or importance.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: The IPanelProps interface provides you with a versatile set of properties to create interactive and dynamic panel components. Whether you're creating collapsible sections, or informative containers, the properties offered by IPanelProps will help you craft panels that enhance the layout and user experience of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IPanelProps

members.description: Control whether the panel is expanded or collapsed. When set to true, the panel is expanded.
members.name: isExpanded

componentInterfaces.description: The IPanelHeaderProps interface provides you with a versatile toolkit to create interactive and dynamic panel headers. Whether you're designing collapsible sections, expandable panels, or informative headers, the properties offered by IPanelHeaderProps will help you craft header components that enhance the usability and aesthetic of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IPanelHeaderProps

members.description: Assign an accent color variant to the panel header, typically used to classify the information level or importance. This accent color can help users quickly identify and associate the header with specific content or actions.
members.name: accent
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: Control whether the panel header is expanded or collapsed. When set to true, the panel header is visually marked as expanded.
members.name: isExpanded

members.description: Display a toggle button on the panel header, allowing users to easily expand or collapse associated content. This toggle button adds interactivity and enhances user control over panel visibility.
members.name: isToggleVisible

componentInterfaces.description: The IPanelBodyProps interface is equipping you with a versatile set of properties to create dynamic and user-centric panel body components. Whether you're displaying content, messages, or data, the properties offered by IPanelBodyProps will help you design body components that enhance the overall user experience of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IPanelBodyProps

members.description: Assign an accent color variant to the panel body, typically used to classify the information level or importance. The accent color helps users quickly identify the significance of the content presented within the body.
members.name: accent
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: Control whether the panel body is visible or hidden. When set to true, the panel body is displayed, providing users with access to its content.
members.name: isVisible

componentInterfaces.description: The IPanelGroupProps interface is providing you with a versatile toolkit to create well-structured and aesthetically appealing panel group components. Whether you're arranging related panels, showcasing multiple sections, or enhancing visual clarity, the properties offered by IPanelGroupProps will help you craft panel groups that effectively organize content in your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IPanelGroupProps

members.description: Enhance the visual organization of panels within the panel group by adding a separator between each panel. This separator serves as a subtle visual cue that distinguishes individual panels.
members.name: hasSeparator

componentInterfaces.description: The IPaperProps interface aims providing you with a robust set of properties to create stylish and functional paper-like components. Whether you're designing cards, sections, or containers, the properties offered by IPaperProps will help you craft paper components that enhance the overall visual and user experience of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IPaperProps

members.description: Create a paper component with sharp edges, giving it a distinctive visual appearance. When set to true, the paper component's corners are sharp, contributing to a more angular and modern design.
members.name: isSharp

componentInterfaces.description: he IProgressProps interface helps providing you with a powerful set of properties to create informative and visually appealing progress indicators. Whether you're displaying loading states, completion percentages, or dynamic data updates, the properties offered by IProgressProps will help you craft progress indicators that enhance the user experience of your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IProgressProps

members.description: Determine the thickness or girth of the progress indicator. The girth enhances the visual presence of the progress indicator, allowing you to adjust its width according to your design preferences.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: Assign a tone to the progress indicator, indicating its significance or status. The tone provides a visual cue that helps users quickly identify the nature of the progress being displayed.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: Specify the percentage value of the progress indicator. This property allows you to convey the completion level of a task or process, providing users with real-time feedback on the progress status.
members.name: value

members.description: Choose a variant for the progress indicator, defining its visual style and animation. The variant options include solid, striped, and indeterminate, each offering a distinct appearance and behavior.
members.name: variant

componentInterfaces.description: The IRadioProps interface is equipping you with a versatile set of properties to create radio button components that facilitate user choices and selections. Whether you're designing dynamic forms, intuitive surveys, or interactive preference settings, the properties offered by IRadioProps will assist you in crafting radio button components that enhance user interaction and data management within your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IRadioProps

members.description: Tailor the layout of the radio button options to align vertically, optimizing space utilization and readability. When set to true, the radio button options are stacked vertically, making it effortless for users to assess and choose from the available selections.
members.name: isVertical

members.description: Assign a value to each radio button option, establishing a meaningful identifier for the selected choice. This value is invaluable for data processing, submission, or programmatic interactions based on user selections.
members.name: value

componentInterfaces.description: The IRadioOptionProps interface helps providing you with a versatile set of properties to create individual radio button option components. Whether you're building complex forms, interactive surveys, or dynamic preference settings, the properties offered by IRadioOptionProps will help you craft radio button options that enhance user interaction and data collection in your application.
componentInterfaces.name: IRadioOptionProps

members.description: Determine the size or girth of the radio option component, enhancing its visual prominence and impact. Choose from a variety of sizes such as 'md', 'lg', 'xl', or 'xxl' to tailor the radio option's appearance to match your application's design aesthetic.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: Assign a color variant to the radio option component, allowing you to classify information based on its importance or level of significance. The status color provides a visual cue that helps users quickly identify the nature of the radio option.
members.name: status

members.description: Set a unique value for the radio option, establishing its identity within a group of options. This value is essential for data processing, capturing user selections, and enabling seamless interaction with the radio option component.
members.name: value

componentInterfaces.description: The ISelectProps interface equips providing you with a powerful set of properties to create customizable and user-friendly select components. Whether you're designing dropdown menus, data filters, or user preference selectors, the properties offered by ISelectProps will assist you in crafting select components that seamlessly integrate into your application's user interface.
componentInterfaces.name: ISelectProps

members.description: Define the size or girth of the select component, influencing its visual prominence and impact. Choose from a range of sizes to tailor the select component's appearance to match your design preferences.
members.name: girth
members.types: xxs | xs | sm | md | lg | xl | xxl

members.description: Assign a color variant to the select component, allowing you to categorize information based on its importance or level of significance. The status color provides a visual cue that helps users quickly identify the nature of the select component's content.
members.name: status

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Stack component.
componentInterfaces.name: IStackProps

members.description: Determines how the items are aligned along the cross-axis of the stack. This property is equivalent to the CSS align-items property.
members.name: align

members.description: Controls whether the stack can wrap its items to the next line. This property is equivalent to the CSS flex-wrap property.
members.name: canFold

members.description: Specifies a React node that will be used as a separator between stack items. This can be any valid React element.
members.name: divider

members.description: Sets the gap (spacing) between stack items. This property is equivalent to the CSS gap property and determines the spacing between adjacent items in the stack.
members.name: gap

members.description: Determines how the items are justified along the main axis of the stack. This property is equivalent to the CSS justify-content property.
members.name: justify
members.types: flex-start | center | flex-end | space-between | space-around | space-evenly | stretch

members.description: Specifies the direction in which the elements are arranged inside the stack. This property controls the order of items and is particularly useful for responsive layouts.
members.name: order

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Switch component.
componentInterfaces.name: ISwitchProps

members.description: Specifies the size of the Switch component. The girth property controls the overall dimensions of the component.
members.name: girth

members.description: Defines the color variant of the Switch component. This property is typically used to visually classify the information based on its level or status. You can use predefined status values (e.g., 'success', 'warning', 'error', etc.) to convey the state of the switch in a meaningful way.
members.name: status

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Table component.
componentInterfaces.name: ITableProps

members.description: Specifies a caption for the table. The caption provides additional context or a brief description of the table's content, helping users understand its purpose or meaning.
members.name: caption

members.description: Defines the spacing between rows in the table. The girth property controls the vertical spacing between rows, enhancing readability.
members.name: girth

members.description: Determines whether the table rows should have a striped appearance. Striped rows alternate background colors between consecutive rows, making it easier for users to distinguish between different rows of data.
members.name: isStriped

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Table Header Cell component (Th).
componentInterfaces.name: IThProps

members.description: Specifies the alignment of the text within the table header cell. The align property controls the horizontal alignment of the text content within the cell.
members.name: align

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Table Data Cell component (Td).
componentInterfaces.name: ITdProps

members.description: Specifies the alignment of the text within the table data cell. The align property controls the horizontal alignment of the text content within the cell.
members.name: align

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props to react to tab changes.
componentInterfaces.name: IWhenTabChangeProps

members.description: Specifies the index of the active tab. This property represents the index of the currently selected tab in a tabbed interface. When provided to a component, the component can react to tab changes and update its content or behavior based on the selected tab. The activeTabIndex can be set to a specific tab index to indicate which tab is currently active. If not provided, the tab index will be 0, indicating that the very first tab is currently active.
members.name: activeTabIndex

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for a group of tabs.
componentInterfaces.name: ITabsGroupProps

members.description: Specifies the index of the active tab within the group. This property determines which tab is initially selected and displayed as active when the TabsGroup component is rendered. The active tab index corresponds to the order of tabs within the group, with indexing starting from 0. If not provided, the very first tab with index 0 will be initially selected as active.
members.name: activeTabIndex

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Tabs component.
componentInterfaces.name: ITabsProps

members.description: Determines whether the Tabs component should justify its content to the full width of the screen. When set to true, the tabs and associated content will expand to fill the available width.
members.name: canJustify

members.description: Determines whether the Tabs component highlights the active tab with a distinct style. When set to true, the active tab will be visually highlighted.
members.name: hasDefaultStyle

members.description: Determines whether the Tabs component should occupy the full width of the screen. When set to true, the entire Tabs component will span the width of its parent container.
members.name: hasFullWidth

members.description: Callback function invoked when a tab is changed. The function is passed an optional object containing the index of the active tab. This allows you to respond to tab changes and update your application's state or perform other actions accordingly.
members.name: whenTabChange

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the container of tab panels.
componentInterfaces.name: ITabPanelsProps

members.description: Determines whether the tab panels should be loaded on-demand. When set to true, the tab panels will only be loaded when their content is needed, potentially improving the initial rendering performance of the Tabs component.
members.name: isLazy

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for an individual tab panel.
componentInterfaces.name: ITabPanelProps

members.description: Determines whether the tab panel should be loaded on-demand. When set to true, the content of the tab panel will only be loaded when it becomes active, potentially improving the initial rendering performance of the Tabs component.
members.name: isLazy

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Tag component, which represents a visually distinct label or marker.
componentInterfaces.name: ITagProps

members.description: The girth property controls the visual size of the Tag component, allowing you to adjust its prominence within its container or context. Use a smaller girth for tags in compact spaces or a larger girth for tags that need to stand out.
members.name: girth

members.description: The icon property enables the addition of an icon to the Tag component, contributing an extra layer of visual information. Enhance the tag's meaning or indicate its category with an associated icon.
members.name: icon

members.description: The isFilled property determines whether the Tag component should have a filled or outlined appearance. Utilize filled tags for emphasis or outlined tags for subtle categorization.
members.name: isFilled

members.description: The tone property lets you apply color variations to the Tag component to convey different levels of importance or meaning. Apply distinct tones to tags representing different priority levels or content categories.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for a group of tags.
componentInterfaces.name: ITagGroupProps

members.description: The girth property controls the overall size of the TagGroup component, affecting its visual presence in the layout. Choose a smaller girth for a compact tag group, or a larger girth for a more prominent tag group.
members.name: girth

members.description: The onAddNewTag callback is triggered when a new tag is added to the group, providing an opportunity to handle the addition. Implement custom logic to respond to tag additions, such as updating data or triggering actions.
members.name: onAddNewTag

members.description: The tone property allows you to customize the color variant of the "add new tag" button. Apply different tones to indicate different actions or emphasize the "add new tag" button.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Text component, representing stylized text content.
componentInterfaces.name: ITextProps

members.description: The bld property controls whether the text should be displayed in bold style. Apply bold formatting to emphasize specific content within the text.
members.name: bld

members.description: The cc property determines whether the text should be displayed in camel case (CamelCase). Utilize camel case text for special formatting or stylistic requirements.
members.name: cc

members.description: The ctr property centers the text horizontally within its container. Center align text to improve visual presentation and layout.
members.name: ctr

members.description: The dsb property displays the text in a disabled state. Indicate that the text is not interactive or temporarily unavailable.
members.name: dsb

members.description: The exd property stretches the text to the full width of its container. Expand text to fill available space, especially in layout designs.
members.name: exd

members.description: The href property specifies a link URL for the text, making it clickable. Create clickable text that navigates to a related page or resource.
members.name: href

members.description: The itl property displays the text in italic style. Apply italics for emphasis or distinct visual styling.
members.name: itl

members.description: The lc property displays the text in lowercase. Convert text to lowercase for consistent styling or formatting.
members.name: lc

members.description: The stk property adds a strikethrough style to the text. Indicate that the text represents content that has been removed or is no longer valid.
members.name: stk

members.description: The tnc property truncates the text inside the width of its parent component. Prevent long text from overflowing and maintain a neat appearance.
members.name: tnc

members.description: The tone property sets the color tone of the text. Apply different color variations to convey meaning or importance.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: The uc property displays the text in uppercase. Convert text to uppercase for headings or branding.
members.name: uc

members.description: The udl property adds an underline style to the text. Highlight text that represents links or interactive elements.
members.name: udl

members.description: The v property specifies the HTML element variant for the text, affecting its semantic meaning. Choose the appropriate HTML element to convey the purpose or importance
members.name: v
members.types: hero | title1 | title2 | subtitle1 | subtitle2 | caption | body1 | body2 | span | p | h1 | h2 | h3 | h4 | h5 | h6

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the DataField component, representing a labeled data entry.
componentInterfaces.name: IDataFieldProps

members.description: The acc property determines the accent color for the field label, visually highlighting it. Apply an accent color to draw attention to the label or differentiate it from other content.
members.name: acc
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: The dsb property controls whether the value of the data field is displayed in a disabled state. Indicate that the data field value is read-only or inactive.
members.name: dsb

members.description: The lbl property specifies the label of the data field, providing context or a title for the associated value. Assign a label to describe the data being displayed, such as "Name", "Age", or "Total Sales".
members.name: lbl

members.description: The tone property sets the color tone of the value in the data field. Apply color variations to convey meaning, significance, or status to the data field value.
members.name: tone
members.types: major | minor | neutral | info | success | warning | danger

members.description: The val property defines the value displayed in the data field, representing associated data or information. Display actual data or content that corresponds to the label, such as a name, number, or custom element.
members.name: val

componentInterfaces.description: Interface for configuring props for the Tooltip component, providing additional information on hover.
componentInterfaces.name: ITooltipProps

members.description: The canWrap property determines whether the tooltip content should wrap to multiple lines if it exceeds the available width. Enable wrapping for lengthy tooltip content to ensure readability and proper presentation.
members.name: canWrap

members.description: The direction property specifies the placement direction of the tooltip relative to its target element. Position the tooltip above, below, to the left, or to the right of the target element as needed.
members.name: direction
